output:
<user-docs>

# Workflow Visualizer

**Story 27-1 ¬∑ Pure Visualizer Function**

The workflow visualizer renders the current state of a codeharness workflow run as a single compact line. It shows where you are in the process ‚Äî which epic, which story, which step is active, what passed, what failed ‚Äî all at a glance.

## What it looks like

```
Epic 17 [3/6] create-b‚úì ‚Üí validat‚Ä¶‚Üí ‚ü≤quality(2/5 1‚úì1‚úó)‚Ä¶ ‚Üí deploy ‚Üí publish
```

Reading left to right:

| Element | Meaning |
|---------|---------|
| `Epic 17 [3/6]` | Working on epic 17, story 3 of 6 |
| `create-b‚úì` | Step "create-brief" completed (truncated to 8 chars) |
| `validat‚Ä¶` | Step "validate" is active (currently running) |
| `‚ü≤quality(2/5 1‚úì1‚úó)‚Ä¶` | Quality gate, iteration 2 of 5, 1 passed, 1 failed |
| `deploy` | Pending step (no marker yet) |
| `publish` | Pending step |

## Status markers

| Marker | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `‚úì` | Done ‚Äî step completed successfully |
| `‚Ä¶` | Active ‚Äî step currently running |
| `‚úó` | Failed ‚Äî step failed |
| `‚äò` | Skipped |
| *(none)* | Pending ‚Äî not yet started |

## Gates

Quality gates have special rendering. An active gate shows its iteration count and verdict tallies:

```
‚ü≤quality(2/5 1‚úì1‚úó)‚Ä¶
```

This means: gate named "quality", on iteration 2 of 5, with 1 pass and 1 failure so far. Completed gates show `‚ü≤quality‚úì`, failed gates show `‚ü≤quality‚úó`.

## Sliding window

When a workflow has more steps than fit on screen, the visualizer collapses steps outside a sliding window:

```
[5‚úì] validate‚Ä¶ ‚Üí deploy ‚Üí publish ‚Üí release ‚Üí notify ‚Üí ‚Ä¶2 more
```

- **`[5‚úì]`** ‚Äî 5 completed steps are collapsed (hidden, all done)
- **`‚Ä¶2 more`** ‚Äî 2 more steps remain after the visible window

The window centers on the active step. By default, up to 5 steps are visible at once.

## Scope prefix

The line starts with context about where you are in the hierarchy:

| Situation | Prefix | Example |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Processing stories in an epic | `Epic {id} [{current}/{total}]` | `Epic 17 [3/6]` |
| Epic-level steps after stories finish | `Epic {id}` | `Epic 17` |
| Flat workflow (no epics) | *(none)* | Steps render directly |

## Stories-done indicator

When all stories in an epic are complete and the workflow moves to epic-level steps, you'll see:

```
Epic 17 stories‚úì ‚Üí deploy‚Ä¶ ‚Üí publish
```

The `stories‚úì` prefix confirms the story loop finished before these steps began.

## Task name truncation

Step names longer than 8 characters are truncated to keep the line compact. For example, `create-story` becomes `create-s` or similar.

## Width enforcement

Output is capped at **80 characters** by default (configurable up to a hard maximum of **120**). If the line would exceed the limit, the visualizer progressively:

1. Shrinks task names (down to 3 characters)
2. Reduces the number of visible step slots
3. Hard-truncates as a last resort

## Configuration

Three settings control the layout:

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---------|---------|-------------|
| `maxWidth` | 80 | Maximum line width in characters (hard cap: 120) |
| `maxStepSlots` | 5 | Maximum number of steps visible before collapsing |
| `taskNameMaxLen` | 8 | Maximum characters per step name before truncation |

## Where it appears

The visualizer output is used by the codeharness status line during workflow execution. It updates as the workflow progresses through epics, stories, gates, and steps ‚Äî giving you a live, single-line view of overall progress without scrolling through logs.

</user-docs>

acceptanceCriteria.description: **Given** the `codeharness` CLI is installed, **When** the user runs `npx codeharness run --help` (or the test suite), **Then** the build succeeds and the visualizer module is loadable ‚Äî `npm run build` exits with code 0. <!-- verification: npm run build exits 0 -->

**Given** a workflow position indicating "processing story 3 of 6 in Epic 17, active on the `implement` step (step index 1 of 4)", **When** the visualize function is called with a maxWidth of 80, **Then** the output string starts with `Epic 17 [3/6]` and contains `impl` (the truncated task name) highlighted as the active step, and the total string length (excluding ANSI escape codes) does not exceed 80 characters. <!-- verification: npx vitest run -- workflow-visualizer ‚Äî test asserts output starts with "Epic 17 [3/6]", contains "impl", and stripped length ‚â§ 80 -->

**Given** a workflow position inside a gate named `quality` at iteration 2 of 5 with 1 check passed and 1 check failed, **When** the visualize function is called, **Then** the output contains the substring `‚ü≤quality(2/5 1‚úì1‚úó)` (or equivalent with the gate name, iteration, and verdict counts). <!-- verification: npx vitest run -- workflow-visualizer ‚Äî test asserts gate rendering substring present -->

**Given** a workflow with 8 total steps where the active step is at index 5 (6th step), **When** the visualize function is called with maxStepSlots of 5, **Then** the output begins with a prefix like `[5‚úì]` (indicating 5 completed steps are collapsed) and ends with a suffix like `‚Üí ‚Ä¶2 more` (indicating 2 remaining steps beyond the window), and shows at most 5 step slots in the visible portion. <!-- verification: npx vitest run -- workflow-visualizer ‚Äî test asserts [N‚úì] prefix, ‚Ä¶N more suffix, ‚â§5 visible slots -->

**Given** a workflow position where all story-level steps are complete and the engine is on epic-level step `deploy`, **When** the visualize function is called, **Then** the output shows `stories‚úì` (or equivalent summary of completed story iteration) followed by the epic-level step rendering. <!-- verification: npx vitest run -- workflow-visualizer ‚Äî test asserts "stories‚úì" appears before epic-level steps -->

**Given** a workflow position in a single-level flow (no nested `for_each`), **When** the visualize function is called, **Then** the output has NO scope prefix ‚Äî it starts directly with the step rendering. <!-- verification: npx vitest run -- workflow-visualizer ‚Äî test asserts no "Epic" prefix for flat flows -->

**Given** a workflow position where the active step has a task name longer than 8 characters (e.g., `create-story`), **When** the visualize function is called with the default taskNameMaxLen of 8, **Then** the task name is truncated to 8 characters or fewer in the output (e.g., `create-s` or `create‚Ä¶`). <!-- verification: npx vitest run -- workflow-visualizer ‚Äî test asserts truncated name ‚â§ 8 chars -->

**Given** any valid workflow position (including edge cases: first step, last step, single step, gate at iteration 0, gate with all checks passed), **When** the visualize function is called with maxWidth of 120, **Then** the output string length (excluding ANSI escape codes) never exceeds 120 characters. <!-- verification: npx vitest run -- workflow-visualizer ‚Äî parametric test over edge cases, all stripped lengths ‚â§ 120 -->

**Given** a workflow position where a step has failed (halted), **When** the visualize function is called, **Then** the failed step is rendered differently from completed and pending steps (e.g., marked with `‚úó` or rendered in a distinct style), and the distinction is visible in plain text output. <!-- verification: npx vitest run -- workflow-visualizer ‚Äî test asserts failed step contains "‚úó" or distinct marker -->

**Given** the full test suite, **When** `npx vitest run` is executed, **Then** all existing tests still pass (zero regressions) AND new tests for the visualizer module pass ‚Äî covering: basic rendering, gate rendering, sliding window, scope prefix, truncation, width limits, edge cases. <!-- verification: npx vitest run exits 0, new test file exists and passes -->
**Given** the `workflow-visualizer.ts` file, **When** its line count is checked, **Then** it is ‚â§ 300 lines (per NFR18). <!-- verification: wc -l src/lib/workflow-visualizer.ts ‚â§ 300 -->

**Given** the visualizer function, **When** called with any input, **Then** it performs no I/O (no file reads, no network calls, no process spawning) ‚Äî it is a pure function from input data to output string. <!-- verification: npx vitest run -- workflow-visualizer ‚Äî test confirms function has no I/O side effects (mocked fs/net unused) -->
